name: Holloman AFB
profile: Military-friendly Holloman AFB housing snapshot anchored to Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and Otero County, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Affordable desert military market with slower velocity, low commute pressure, and strong payment accessibility

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 New Mexico VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Holloman is a low-entry-price market, but buyers should still model desert utilities, roof condition, HVAC, wind/dust exposure, and resale depth.
Lower home prices can make ownership accessible, but the strongest decision depends on assignment length, property condition, neighborhood fit, and household lifestyle needs.
Alamogordo is the primary housing lane, while Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and Las Cruces-area alternatives each create different commute and lifestyle tradeoffs.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for practical Holloman / Alamogordo-area housing, including New Mexico tax and insurance assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_alamogordo_otero_county: Alamogordo and Otero County require neighborhood-level screening. Military families should compare exact address, school zone, commute, property condition, desert exposure, and resale logic before committing.

school_quality.on_base: Holloman Elementary may serve some base-connected families; eligibility and attendance boundaries should be verified by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Alamogordo Public Schools, Tularosa, Cloudcroft, and surrounding district options vary by address. Families should verify exact assignment, commute, and program fit before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by military turnover, local civilian employment, limited family-size supply, and the depth of the Alamogordo market.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate for 3-bedroom military-family rentals in the Holloman / Alamogordo area.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Mission-focused desert Air Force community with low commute stress, affordable housing, outdoor access, and a smaller-market lifestyle centered on Alamogordo and Otero County.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Holloman is one of the more affordable Air Force markets by purchase price, but the right move still depends on property condition, assignment length, desert utilities, family lifestyle fit, and resale depth.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and select commute alternatives by all-in payment, condition, school needs, and exit strategy.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Holloman gives military families strong payment accessibility and low commute pressure, but the best housing move depends on property condition, desert lifestyle fit, resale depth, and assignment length.

summary_points: Holloman AFB sits inside an affordable Alamogordo/Otero County housing market with low commute stress and strong practical ownership potential.
Alamogordo is the primary housing lane, while Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and Las Cruces-area alternatives create different lifestyle and commute tradeoffs.
Low purchase prices can support strong VA-buying math, but property condition, resale depth, assignment length, and desert maintenance must still be tested.
Families should compare school zones, spouse employment, healthcare access, childcare, and amenities before choosing a location.
The best Holloman housing decision balances BAH, all-in cost, commute, lifestyle fit, property condition, and expected exit strategy.
opportunities: Lower purchase prices than most U.S. military markets.
Low commute pressure can improve quality of life.
VA buyers may find practical ownership paths when debt and target price are controlled.
Alamogordo offers daily-service access close to base.
Tularosa, La Luz, and Cloudcroft can provide lifestyle alternatives for buyers who understand commute and property tradeoffs.
risks: Resale depth can be thinner than larger metros.
Rural/desert lifestyle may not fit every family.
Older housing stock can create HVAC, roof, plumbing, electrical, and insulation exposure.
Wind, dust, heat, monsoon drainage, and wildfire-adjacent risk should be considered.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
buyer_notes: Holloman buyers should compare Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and select Las Cruces alternatives by commute, schools, condition, lifestyle, and all-in payment.
Do not buy simply because the home price looks low; test resale, property condition, neighborhood, and assignment length.
A clean, well-maintained home with good HVAC, roof, insulation, and resale logic may be better than the cheapest option.
Cloudcroft can offer cooler mountain lifestyle but has very different weather, commute, and maintenance considerations.
This market rewards buyers who preserve residual income and avoid assuming fast appreciation.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around commute, VA readiness, condition, HVAC, roof, cooling efficiency, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes near Holloman or strong Alamogordo corridors can appeal to military families.
Military buyers respond well to clear roof/HVAC information, clean inspections, and PCS-friendly timing.
Homes with strong condition and low-maintenance desert features may stand out more than homes relying only on low price.
investor_angles: Military renter demand can support stable occupancy for clean, well-located homes.
Low acquisition cost can improve cash-flow potential.
Investors should stress-test vacancy, maintenance, tenant turnover, HVAC replacement, and smaller-market resale.
Best suited for yield-focused investors who understand base-driven demand and conservative underwriting.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and extended commute alternatives by commute, school zone, safety, utilities, and property condition.
Verify roof age, HVAC condition, insulation, windows, drainage, cooling efficiency, utility expectations, and wind/dust exposure before finalizing a purchase budget.
Avoid choosing solely by lowest price if the home has weak condition or limited resale logic.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, desert utility pressure, and assignment-length exposure.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and local inventory.
Highlight commute logic, VA readiness, roof condition, HVAC quality, cooling efficiency, and low-maintenance desert features.
Homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, insulation, and clean inspection history should be positioned clearly.
Military buyers need practical value and confidence in monthly cost, not generic listing language.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can support stable occupancy near Holloman AFB.
Single-family rentals in good condition and practical commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should model HVAC maintenance, vacancy, turnover, utility efficiency, and repairs conservatively.
Clean, functional homes near base, services, and schools may outperform cheaper but weaker-condition inventory.

market.market_type_summary: Affordable Alamogordo military market with slower velocity, low commute pressure, and desert-condition ownership considerations

base_profile.installation_type: Training / Test / Remotely Piloted Aircraft / Fighter and Mission Support Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 49th Wing / Air Education and Training Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Holloman AFB is a major Air Force installation in southern New Mexico supporting training, test, remotely piloted aircraft operations, mission support, and aircrew readiness in the Tularosa Basin near White Sands.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Desert, affordable, mission-focused, commute-light, smaller-market, family-sensitive, outdoorsy, and strongly tied to Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, and Otero County housing choices.
base_profile.base_bluf: Holloman is not just a low-cost housing decision. It is a desert-lifestyle, property-condition, HVAC, assignment-length, school-zone, and resale-depth decision. The low prices can be powerful, but the household still needs a clean exit strategy.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, spouse/family lifestyle fit, commute simplicity, HVAC and roof condition, BAH fit, and resale risk before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize affordability, short commute, rental flexibility, roommate options, and access to daily services in Alamogordo.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Holloman housing conversations are about low payment, realistic resale, desert maintenance, neighborhood confidence, property condition, and assignment length.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Holloman is influenced by military turnover, limited quality inventory, affordable acquisition costs, and the need for clean, functional homes near Alamogordo and base commute corridors.

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment
on_base_housing.housing_office_hours: Monday-Friday, 7:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. MST; verify current hours
on_base_housing.map_zone: housing support area / Soaring Heights community
best_for: Families wanting to reduce rural-market uncertainty
Users who want predictable access to base services
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base affordability may be strong enough that families should compare both paths carefully
Desert utilities, HVAC condition, cooling costs, and resale depth should be modeled for off-base purchase scenarios
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around Holloman, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, desert utility exposure, HVAC condition, family support needs, pet needs, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Alamogordo Practical Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Primary housing lane for Holloman families with short commute, daily services, and broadest local inventory.
best_for: Users wanting services and short commute
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address and gate
likely_gate_logic: Other gates depending on duty location and current access rules
watchouts: Neighborhood and property condition vary
Older homes may require stronger HVAC and roof inspection
School assignment should be verified by exact address
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for most users because it balances commute, services, affordability, and inventory.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: North Alamogordo
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical Alamogordo corridor with access to services and potentially stronger family fit depending on exact pocket.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-30 minutes depending on exact address, route, and gate
watchouts: Property age and condition vary
Cooling and roof condition should be checked
Exact school zone should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good family and buyer corridor when the property is condition-strong and payment remains controlled.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: South Alamogordo / Boles Acres
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Closer-to-base practical option with short commute potential and affordability.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-20 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Neighborhood quality should be checked at the block level
Older homes may require maintenance reserves
Do not choose solely based on proximity
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good for commute-first users if the exact property condition and neighborhood fit are strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Tularosa
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Small-town option north of Alamogordo with affordability and quiet lifestyle, but commute and services should be reviewed.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-40 minutes depending on exact address, route, and gate
watchouts: Longer commute than Alamogordo
Inventory may be limited
Utility, school, and service access should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good value option for users who intentionally want quieter small-town living.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: La Luz / High Rolls
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Scenic rural-edge option with space and mountain foothill character, but property and utility due diligence matters.
best_for: Experienced buyers comfortable with rural details
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-45 minutes depending on exact address, road conditions, and gate
watchouts: Well, septic, internet, roads, and utilities should be verified
Fire/wildfire-adjacent risk may matter
Resale market may be more specialized
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for users who intentionally want rural or foothill living and understand maintenance/resale tradeoffs.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Cloudcroft
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Cooler mountain-lifestyle option with strong scenery and tourism appeal, but very different commute, weather, and maintenance profile.
best_for: Buyers comfortable with snow and rural services
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 45-65+ minutes depending on exact address, road conditions, weather, and gate
watchouts: Longer commute can become draining
Winter weather is different from Alamogordo
Insurance, septic, well, internet, roof, and wildfire risk should be checked
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Only recommended when the household intentionally wants mountain living and accepts commute, weather, and maintenance tradeoffs.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Las Cruces Extended Commute
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Larger-city alternative with more services and employment options, but commute to Holloman is substantial.
best_for: Families with specific lifestyle or school needs
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 70-90+ minutes depending on exact address, route, weather, and gate
watchouts: Commute may overpower lifestyle benefits
Fuel and time cost should be included
Only makes sense for specific household reasons
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Use carefully. Las Cruces can solve lifestyle or employment needs, but the commute is not a casual tradeoff.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Very favorable inside Alamogordo and base-adjacent areas, but more complicated from Tularosa, Cloudcroft, La Luz, or Las Cruces alternatives.
primary_commute_risks: Choosing by lowest price instead of property condition
Longer drives from Cloudcroft or Las Cruces
Desert wind, dust, monsoon drainage, and road visibility
planning_guidance: Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Confirm which gate best supports the user’s duty location.
Compare Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and extended alternatives by route reliability, services, schools, and property condition.
Include fuel, time, utilities, HVAC, and maintenance exposure in the housing decision.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: Holloman commute risk is low if the user lives near Alamogordo, but rural, mountain, or larger-city alternatives can quietly turn savings into time, fuel, and maintenance cost.

bah_market_reality.overall_position: Strongly favorable for disciplined buyers, with rural-market caution.
bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: Low purchase prices give many Holloman families realistic buying power, especially compared with most Air Force markets.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for short assignments, users uncertain about desert lifestyle fit, or households that do not want resale exposure.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Holloman should be modeled as a strong affordability market, but PCSUnited should still test rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, timeline, family needs, lifestyle fit, and exit plan before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Outdoor access to White Sands and mountain areas
On-base housing can simplify PCS logistics
watchouts: Rural/desert lifestyle may not fit every family
School assignments and program fit should be verified
Spouse employment options may be more limited than larger metros
Healthcare and specialty services may require planning
Older homes can increase HVAC and maintenance exposure
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Holloman can work well for families who value affordability, simplicity, and outdoor access, but it requires honest lifestyle screening and a clear housing exit strategy.

arrival_checklist: Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Holloman AFB sources.
Contact the Holloman AFB Military Housing Office or Housing Referral Office early for current local housing conditions and relocation support.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, spouse employment, and expected assignment length.
Compare Alamogordo, Tularosa, La Luz, Cloudcroft, and other alternatives using commute, schools, safety, services, and all-in monthly cost.
Review roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, insulation, drainage, internet availability, cooling efficiency, and wind/dust-related maintenance exposure before buying.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, and exit risk before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not buy only because the price is low.
Do not ignore resale risk in a smaller desert market.
Do not assume every affordable property is a good VA purchase.
Verify schools, internet, utilities, cooling costs, and property condition before committing.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
Lifestyle fit matters heavily at Holloman because larger-city amenities are limited.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with affordability, exit strategy, property condition, commute simplicity, desert maintenance, and lifestyle fit. Holloman clients need grounded guidance, not hype.
strong_listing_angles: Cooling efficiency and low-maintenance desert features
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, utilities, and maintenance
Alamogordo versus Tularosa versus Cloudcroft tradeoffs
Property condition and VA appraisal readiness
Future resale appeal to military buyers
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Holloman realtor should sound like a payment strategist, property-condition filter, and desert-market exit planner — not just a sales agent.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Holloman base orientation map is created.
Holloman-specific public market data is thinner than larger metros, so Alamogordo and Otero County context should be used carefully.

source_notes: Use official Holloman AFB sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Air Force Housing, Holloman Military Housing Office, Housing Referral Office, and Soaring Heights sources for on-base housing and privatized housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Alamogordo-area MLS, Zillow-style indexes, Realtor.com-style market data, rental listing aggregators, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, insurance, school-zone, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, utility costs, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, Beale, Cannon, Charleston, Davis-Monthan, Dover, Dyess, Eglin, Elmendorf, F.E. Warren, and Fairchild.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for Holloman AFB.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.
